○ lead-i18 · assign_scenarios: shopsystem-messaging — shop-msg implicit CWD-based shop resolution   [● P1 · OPEN]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-27 · Updated: 2026-05-27

DESCRIPTION
Add implicit shop resolution to shop-msg: when called without --bc/--lead, walk up from CWD looking for .claude/shop/name.md and .claude/shop/type.md. Read type.md to choose BC vs lead mode; read name.md to resolve the canonical name; use the registry to find the path as today. Explicit --bc <name> / --lead <name> remain unchanged for cross-shop addressing. This eliminates the need for the canonical BC settings.json SessionStart hook to embed the shop name. Anchors: PDR-007 (name-based addressing — this extends it with a CWD-implicit form, does not reverse it). Prior art: bd discovers .beads/ from CWD using the same walk-up pattern. Discriminator: NEW capability — implicit CWD resolution does not exist today; calling shop-msg prime with no addressing flags currently errors via the mutually-exclusive arg group. Vehicle: assign_scenarios. PO must author scenarios pinning: (a) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a BC when CWD is inside a BC shop dir, (b) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a lead when CWD is inside a lead shop dir, (c) error case when CWD is not inside any shop, (d) explicit --bc/--lead still works unchanged. Suggested generalization to other subcommands (pending, respond, watch) — PO judgment whether to bundle.

NOTES
PO authored PDR-009 (implicit CWD-based shop resolution) and 9 Gherkin scenarios in features/messaging-registry/ (29-37) pinning: bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves BC + lead from CWD via walk-up of .claude/shop/{name,type}.md (29, 30); nearest-shop precedence on nested trees (31); clear non-zero diagnostic when no shop ancestor exists (32); rejection of partial shop marker — name.md without type.md or vice versa (33); unregistered-derived-name produces same diagnostic shape as explicit-flag unregistered (34); explicit --bc/--lead always wins over implicit-CWD (35); scenario outline applying the implicit lookup to pending/read/respond/watch (36); 'send' resolves SENDER implicitly but RECIPIENT remains explicit (37). PDR-009 commits the architectural rationale (single source of truth for shop identity; preserves PDR-003 alt F byte-for-byte pour; mirrors bd's CWD walk-up; PDR-007 compatible). PDR-009 also commits the scope of bundling (all subcommands taking --bc/--lead get implicit lookup; recipient on send is always explicit). Architect dispatches next after BC pre-state verification.

```text
○ lead-8z1 · request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — allow multiple clarify rows per work_id in outbox   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-19 · Updated: 2026-05-19

DESCRIPTION
The postgres UNIQUE constraint on (bc, work_id, direction, message_type) prevents a BC from filing a second clarify response on the same work_id. This is an implementation artifact — §5 of the spec places no cardinality constraint on clarify messages. Multi-turn clarify exchanges (BC asks, lead answers, BC asks again) are a legitimate protocol flow but are currently blocked, forcing workarounds (mechanism_observation, fresh work_id). Fix: relax or drop the uniqueness on message_type within the outbox direction, or scope the constraint to only message types where one-per-work_id is actually required (work_done).
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
○ lead-rv9 · work_done(blocked) protocol gap — lead-side response shape unspecified   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-12 · Updated: 2026-05-12

DESCRIPTION
Surfaced 2026-05-12 by lead-231.2's blocked work_done — the first natural occurrence of the work_done(blocked) branch from from-prototype-1.md finding 3 and §8 (documented in bc_reviewer_prompt.md but unexercised).

The protocol defines that the Reviewer can emit work_done(blocked) when the implementation has a gap (not a scenario gap, which would be clarify). What it does NOT define is the lead-side response. The Reviewer's report names what's wrong; what message does the lead send back to get it fixed?

Options considered for lead-231.2's specific case:
- request_bugfix: tightens unpinned behavior via scenarios. Awkward — the gap is a hash-value defect on the BC's internal feature files, not a missing scenario pin.
- request_maintenance: flat change (value-only update of 4 specific hashes). Best fit for this concrete case; chosen for lead-231.2 follow-up.
- clarify: for asking questions, not for 'redo your work'. Doesn't fit.
- No message; BC re-submits unilaterally: protocol doesn't authorize BC to re-emit work_done without lead input.

What needs designing:
1. Spec-level: does the existing 7-type catalog cover work_done(blocked) responses adequately by composition (e.g., 'always request_maintenance for value-only fixes the Reviewer specified'), or is a new mechanism needed?
2. Template-level: bc_reviewer prompt should constrain the work_done(blocked) report shape so the lead can route deterministically (Reviewer cites specific defect + canonical fix → lead dispatches request_maintenance carrying the fix).
3. Discriminator-level: 'Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' becomes an explicit pathway under request_maintenance, not just refactor/doc/value-update.

Also worth noting the substantive Implementer defect that triggered this: Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values rather than running scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the modified files. Reviewer caught it. §4.4 loop validation on a real Implementer error — the kind synthetic prototypes never produced.

Related: lead-231 (parent brief), lead-231.2 (the blocked dispatch), findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 3 / §8 (the prior 'documented but unexercised' note).
```
</details></tool-use>

## Decision summary for Stream 1

I'll choose **hybrid (b) + ADR-tracked (a)**:
- **Now (operational)**: canonize the workaround — lead dispatches a fresh `request_bugfix` (or `request_maintenance` for non-bug clarifies) on a new lead-side bead carrying the resolving scenario set, referencing the original work_id in its body. Already de-facto precedent (lead-bxl).
- **Future (durable)**: ADR records a future `shop-msg send clarify_response` primitive blocked on the storage-collision-by-message-type widening (which already exists as `allow_multi_type` for BC-to-lead writes; the gap is exposing it on the lead-to-BC send path and adding the catalog primitive).

The `lead-d6o`, `lead-8z1`, `lead-rv9`, and `lead-4wy` beads already document the underlying gaps. I'll cite them in the ADR rather than redundantly file.

Let me draft ADR-009 (next number after 008; PDR-009 has the same digit but ADRs are independent numbering).

 `./adr/008-docs-bc-introduction.md`

```text
1	# ADR-008 — shopsystem-docs as a new Bounded Context
2	
3	**Status:** accepted (2026-05-22)
4	**Authors:** dstengle, Claude (lead-architect)
5	**Anchored to:** [brief 007](../briefs/007-end-user-adoption-documentation.md)
6	(resolved Q4); [ADR-004](004-bc-launcher-as-new-bc.md) (precedent for
7	introducing a new BC under the shopsystem product).
8	**Depends on:** [ADR-001](001-framework-packaging.md)
9	
10	## Decision
11	
12	A new Bounded Context, `shopsystem-docs`, is created to own end-user adoption
13	documentation for the shopsystem framework. It is the outward-facing doc surface
14	an adopter encounters when they want to use the framework to build their own
15	product. It is not folded into any existing BC, and it does not live in this
16	lead-shop repository.
17	
18	The BC follows standard shopsystem BC mechanisms: scenarios are authored by the
19	lead-shop PO and dispatched via `assign_scenarios`; the BC has its own
20	`bc-implementer` / `bc-reviewer` cycle. The lead shop dispatches scenarios for
21	doc work the same way it dispatches scenarios for `shopsystem-messaging`,
22	`shopsystem-bc-launcher`, etc.
23	
24	**v1 publishing format is plain markdown** (brief 007 boundary 10). The first
25	implementation of `shopsystem-docs` ships plain `.md` files committed to the
26	BC's repo; the adopter reads them directly (GitHub's rendered markdown view,
27	or raw markdown in an editor). v1 does NOT ship a MkDocs/Docusaurus/static-site-
28	generator build, does NOT ship GitHub Pages or any other hosted rendered HTML,
29	and does NOT ship a custom rendering pipeline. This narrows the BC's v1 scope
30	to content authoring; documentation-site tooling is explicitly deferred to a
31	future iteration once the markdown content has proven its shape.
32	
33	## Alternatives considered
34	
35	**Option B — Docs as a directory in the lead-shop repo (`shopsystem-product/docs/`).**
36	The lead shop already holds briefs, ADRs, PDRs, and the canonical scenario
37	register; adoption docs could be one more directory alongside them. Rejected
38	per brief 007 boundary 1: stakeholder explicitly ruled "new repo" and the
39	audience split is load-bearing. The lead-shop repo's contents serve internal
40	contributors (briefs, ADRs, PDRs, the spec); the adopter is a different
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-27 16:49:36Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# ADR-009 — Clarify-resolution vehicle: re-dispatch via existing catalog message types (with future `clarify_response` primitive deferred)

**Status:** accepted (2026-05-27)
**Authors:** dstengle, Claude (lead-architect)
**Anchored to:** [§4 spec catalog](../04-message-catalog.md) (eight message
types, no `clarify_response`); [§4.4 closure narrative](../04-message-catalog.md);
[findings/from-prototype-1.md §5](../findings/from-prototype-1.md) (vehicle
selection by pre-state).
**Related beads:** `lead-d6o` (clarify protocol gap), `lead-8z1` (multiple
clarify rows per work_id), `lead-rv9` (work_done(blocked) lead-side response),
`lead-4wy` (catalog work_id pattern asymmetry).
**Depends on:** [ADR-006](006-messaging-name-registry-design.md) (the
message-registry shape into which a future primitive would slot).

## Context

When a BC emits `clarify` on a lead dispatch, the spec's §4 catalog lists no
`clarify_response` message type. Empirically verified 2026-05-27:

\```
$ shop-msg send --help
usage: shop-msg send [-h]
                     {request_maintenance,assign_scenarios,request_bugfix} ...
\```

— `shop-msg send` exposes only the three outbound vehicles a lead uses to
introduce or tighten work. `shop-msg respond clarify` is BC-side direction
(the BC writes its question into the lead's inbox), confirmed by:

\```
$ shop-msg respond clarify --help
usage: shop-msg respond clarify [-h] --bc BC --work-id WORK_ID
                                --question QUESTION
\```

The `--bc` flag names the BC writing the response — i.e., the caller is the
BC, not the lead. The CLI has no lead-side counterpart to "answer a clarify
in-band."

Compounding the gap: even if a `clarify_response` primitive existed,
`repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/shop_msg/storage.py:210-223` enforces a
strict "one inbox row per (bc, work_id)" gate on the lead-to-BC direction
when `allow_multi_type=False` (the default for sends). The storage layer
already carries an `allow_multi_type` flag (lines 178, 186-191) which is set
by BC-to-lead writes so multiple message types (`work_done`,
`mechanism_observation`, `clarify`) can coexist for the same work_id in the
lead inbox; the lead-to-BC send path does not currently opt into that
relaxation, so a hypothetical `clarify_response` row would collide with the
original `assign_scenarios` row on the same `work_id`.

The de-facto precedent for resolving a BC clarify is already established:
the `lead-bxl` bead (closed 2026-05-22) resolved
`shopsystem-templates-918`'s BC clarify by dispatching a fresh
`request_bugfix` on a new lead-side bead carrying the resolving scenario
(scenario 08, missing-`.claude.json` warn-and-proceed). The original `lead-gua`
(work_id `shopsystem-templates-918`) `assign_scenarios` row remains in
flight on the wire; the new bugfix is *additive*, not a replacement. See the
`lead-d6o` bead's NOTES entry (2026-05-22) for the empirical confirmation
that this pattern works end-to-end.

## Decision

Adopt a two-layer position:

**Layer (b) — operational, in effect immediately.** The lead resolves a BC
`clarify` by dispatching a fresh outbound message on a *new lead-side bead*,
carrying the resolving scenario set or maintenance instruction in the
appropriate vehicle per the standard discriminator:

- BC clarify pertaining to **unpinned existing behavior** → `request_bugfix`
  on a new lead bead, carrying the tightening scenario(s).
- BC clarify pertaining to **new capability** the original dispatch did not
  yet name → `assign_scenarios` on a new lead bead, carrying the additional
  scenarios.
- BC clarify pertaining to a **flat correction** with no scenario change →
  `request_maintenance` on a new lead bead.

The dispatch description must reference the original work_id (the one that
elicited the clarify) so the BC can correlate. The new bead's ID is the
work_id on the wire. The original dispatch remains in flight; the BC's
existing clarify row in the lead inbox is consumed by the architect once
the resolving dispatch is on the wire (per existing
`shop-msg consume` discipline).

**Layer (a) — durable, deferred to a future implementation.** A
`shop-msg send clarify_response --bc <name> --work-id <id> --resolution <text>
[--scenario-file ...]` primitive is the longer-term shape. It requires:

1. A new `ClarifyResponse` Pydantic schema in `src/catalog/`.
2. The lead-to-BC send path opting into `allow_multi_type=True` for
   `clarify_response` so the row can coexist with the original
   `assign_scenarios` / `request_bugfix` / `request_maintenance` row on the
   same work_id.
3. A BC-side semantics decision: does receipt of `clarify_response` re-open
   the original dispatch for re-processing, or does it stand alone as a
   pinned answer the BC reads alongside the original? The `lead-d6o` bead
   itemizes this design question; the answer dictates whether the original
   row's lifecycle changes when the response lands.

Path (a) is not blocked on path (b); they are complementary. Path (a) is
deferred because the storage gate relaxation, the catalog schema addition,
and the BC-side re-processing semantics are a coordinated change that wants
its own brief/PDR cycle, and the operational pressure to ship it has not
yet exceeded the friction of path (b).

## Alternatives considered

**Option A — Implement `shop-msg send clarify_response` now.** Rejected for
the immediate window. The change is non-trivial (catalog schema + storage
gate widening + BC-side semantics + tests) and the existing workaround is
already in routine use without observable failure. Deferring buys time to
batch this with the other catalog-protocol-gap beads (`lead-d6o`,
`lead-8z1`, `lead-rv9`, `lead-4wy`) into a coordinated brief revision.

**Option C — New message type `clarify_resolved` signaling "process the
originally-dispatched scenarios now."** Listed as design question #3 in
`lead-d6o`. Rejected as the standalone vehicle for this ADR because it
overlaps with option A; either path subsumes it. Folded into the future
brief that resolves path (a).

**Option D — Carry the original scenarios forward in every follow-up
dispatch.** Avoids the storage-collision concern by reusing the existing
vehicles' carry-forward semantics. Rejected because it produces wire-form
hash drift between dispatches (the feature-title wrapping changes between
canonical renders), making BDD attestation harder to track and inflating
the on-wire payload. `lead-d6o` documents the hash-drift symptom from
`lead-1pi` (scenario 57 hash was `d4b8aac19b34ea8c` in `lead-xkh`,
`1621b59b0ea8b20b` in `lead-1pi`).

## Consequences

- The de-facto pattern already in use (per `lead-bxl`) is now canonical.
  Future lead-shop turns resolving a BC clarify file a new lead-side bead
  and dispatch via the appropriate existing vehicle; this is no longer a
  "workaround," it is the documented procedure until path (a) ships.
- Lead-shop role templates (`lead-architect.md`) should learn the
  clarify-resolution dispatch shape: cite the original work_id in the new
  bead's description, scope as "additive — original dispatch remains in
  flight," and use the standard discriminator on the resolving content.
  A follow-up bead tracks this template edit.
- The storage gate at `storage.py:210-223` is the load-bearing dependency
  for path (a). Any future PDR that proposes a `clarify_response` primitive
  must widen this gate or set `allow_multi_type=True` for the new
  message type's lead-to-BC writes.
- The `lead-d6o` bead is **not closed** by this ADR. It tracks the deeper
  design question of multi-turn clarify semantics; this ADR commits the
  operational vehicle but defers the underlying protocol shape.
- `mechanism_observation` from a BC reporting a different kind of
  protocol gap (e.g., the cross-tracker work_id pattern observed with
  `shopsystem-templates-918`) is unaffected by this decision; it routes
  per the standing rules in the lead-primer.

```

bd create "mechanism_observation: cross-tracker work_id pattern (shopsystem-templates-918 from shopsystem-bc-launcher)" --priority 3 --description "Observed during shopsystem-templates-918 clarify drain (2026-05-22, reconfirmed 2026-05-27): the work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' is prefixed with one BC's tracker name (shopsystem-templates) while the actual sender of the assign_scenarios was a different BC (shopsystem-bc-launcher). 'bd search 918' returns no lead-side bead; the lead-gua bead's NOTES treat 918 as a BC-side tracker ID.

This is a cross-tracker work_id pattern: the on-wire work_id is the issuing BC's bead ID, not the lead's bead ID. §6 of the spec names the lead's bead ID as the canonical work_id, so this is an asymmetry worth pinning.

Related: lead-4wy (Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept). That bead documents catalog-schema validation asymmetry; this bead documents the *source-of-truth* asymmetry — which tracker's beads supply the work_id when the dispatch crosses BCs without going through the lead.

In this specific case the dispatch was actually lead-side (lead-gua) but the on-wire work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' was set to the BC-side tracker reference. Whether that was an intentional cross-reference or a misuse is unclear from the surviving evidence.

A formal mechanism_observation cannot be emitted from the lead (the respond subcommands are BC-side). This bead is the lead-side equivalent record. If a future BC re-encounters the same pattern, it should emit a mechanism_observation through 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' citing this bead.

No immediate action required; observation only." 2>&1
```

```text
# Adopting the shopsystem framework

This walkthrough is the entry document for an adopter of the shopsystem
framework. It is designed to be read in order; each section names a move
the adopter performs on their own host. The walkthrough ends at the
[Bootstrapped](#bootstrapped) section, which is the **terminal section**
designating completion.

## Prerequisites

- A host with Docker and the `shop-msg` CLI installed (or available via
  the canonical devcontainer).
- A Claude Code installation capable of opening a session against a
  container.
- An adopter manifest listing the lead shop and the BCs the adopter
  intends to operate.

## Move 1 — Bring the lead shop up

Start the lead shop container on the adopter's host. The image, compose
file, and run command are documented in the devcontainer BC; the
walkthrough does not duplicate them. After this move, the lead shop
container is running locally.

## Move 2 — Register the BCs

For each BC the adopter's manifest declares, register the BC's container
with the messaging registry. Use `shop-msg registry register` (one
invocation per BC) so that subsequent dispatches can address each BC by
its canonical name.

## Move 3 — Open a Claude Code session and dispatch

Open a Claude Code session against the running lead-shop container. From
the session, use `shop-msg send` to dispatch a first `assign_scenarios`
message to one of the adopter's BCs. This proves the round-trip path is
live on the adopter's host.

## Bootstrapped

This is the terminal section. The adoption walkthrough designates
**successful completion of the three executable steps below** as what
"the framework is bootstrapped" means in v1.

1. **Bring a lead-shop container up on the adopter's host.** Run the
   lead-shop image so that `docker ps` lists the lead-shop container as
   running on the adopter's host.
2. **Register each BC the adopter's manifest declares with the messaging
   registry, such that `shop-msg registry list` reports both the lead
   and each BC by name.** Invoke `shop-msg registry register` once per
   manifest entry; then run `shop-msg registry list` and confirm the
   lead and every declared BC appear in its output.
3. **Dispatch an `assign_scenarios` message from a Claude Code session
   opened against the lead-shop container to one of the adopter's BCs.**
   Open a Claude Code session against the running lead-shop container
   and run `shop-msg send assign_scenarios ...` targeting one of the
   adopter's BCs; observe the message arrive in that BC's inbox.

When all three moves have been performed successfully on the adopter's
host, the framework is bootstrapped in v1.
```
</details></tool-use>

Also check whether the BC has them on a working branch or staged-uncommitted (the prior hygiene observation noted uncommitted work in the BC).

2. **Pick the vehicle to close the gap.** Options:
   - (a) Fresh `assign_scenarios` on a new lead-side bead carrying the 4 unpinned scenarios. Treats them as if newly dispatched (cleanest; avoids litigating whether the BC's earlier work_done was valid).
   - (b) `request_bugfix` on a new lead-side bead carrying the 4 scenarios, framed as "capability claimed in prior work_done but unpinned in features/". More accurate to the situation; surfaces protocol discipline.
   - (c) If `request_shop_card` is a real `shop-msg send` vehicle (not hypothetical), use it to first read the BC's authoritative register before deciding. Confirm with `shop-msg send --help` whether it exists.

The BC genuinely didn't pin them; "capability exists but unpinned" is technically right but pragmatically the BC has no implementation to fix, just net-new pinning to do. But you make the call as architect.

3. **ADR-009 v2 — write it.** The prior reconciliation surfaced this v2 candidate: ADR-009 as canonized (vehicle b — request_bugfix on a fresh bead for clarify-resolution) does not unambiguously specify the scope of a clarify-resolution `work_done.scenario_hashes`. Spell out the contract:

That's BC-side discipline, not directly the lead's to enforce — but the ADR-009 v2 clarification implicitly tightens what counts as a valid `work_done`. Inspect bd issues

`bd show lead-apk 2>&1 && echo "---" && bd show lead-km9 2>&1`

```text
○ lead-apk · Reconcile lead-km9: BC delivered only the replacement scenario (lead-b34) — 4 of 5 original scenarios still unpinned in shopsystem-docs   [● P1 · OPEN]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-27 · Updated: 2026-05-27

DESCRIPTION
  (none)

NOTES
Reconciliation finding from lead-b34 work_done (2026-05-27).

ORIGINAL DISPATCH (lead-km9, 2026-05-22): assign_scenarios carrying 5 scenarios to shopsystem-docs:
  - 7ac28115fe1a2a33 (entry doc)
  - b0fe0666bcaa2398 (end-to-end runtime — superseded by lead-b34)
  - 6055501e8a23a652 (plain markdown only)
  - 8c55526c7a83bcd0 (agent-consumable walkthrough structure)
  - 3874a7f3c34e4f60 (launcher gap honestly named)

BC's clarify on lead-km9 explained that b0fe0666 was not establishable in its pytest-bdd harness, but said nothing about the other four being blocked. PO + Architect dispatched lead-b34 carrying only the b0fe0666 replacement; the other four were expected to land via the original lead-km9 dispatch.

OBSERVED STATE (BC repo /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs):
  - features/ contains exactly ONE file: adoption_walkthrough_terminal_section.feature, pinning 56babfaeec4514a3 only.
  - grep '@scenario_hash' in BC features/ returns only 56babfaeec4514a3.
  - BC work_done echoes the 4 other hashes, but they are not in the BC's features/ tree.
  - lead-km9 has no recorded work_done in the BC outbox (shop-msg read outbox for lead-km9 returns only the BC's clarify).

INTERPRETATION: per ADR-009 vehicle (b), the BC was expected to emit ONE consolidated work_done covering 56babfaeec4514a3 (the lead-b34 replacement) plus the four unchanged originals — addressed to the lead-b34 work_id. The BC did echo all five hashes in scenario_hashes:, but in fact only landed the replacement; the four others were not implemented. The work_done's hash list misrepresents BC state.

POSSIBLE CAUSES:
  1. BC understood the dispatch as ONLY covering the replacement (lead-b34 description framed it as additive — 'carries only the revised scenario'). BC may have inferred that the other four would be tracked via a separate work_done on lead-km9, and is still in flight on those.
  2. BC mechanically copied the hash list from the lead-b34 dispatch payload into work_done.scenario_hashes without verifying each hash had a corresponding feature file. (ADR-009 v2 candidate: tighten the BC-side requirement that scenario_hashes in work_done must be observably pinned in features/.)

FOLLOW-UP OPTIONS:
  (a) Send request_shop_card to shopsystem-docs to read its authoritative scenario register — confirms what BC believes it has pinned.
  (b) If BC is genuinely still in flight on the four originals, leave lead-km9 open and wait.
  (c) If BC considers lead-km9 closed (because lead-b34 work_done echoed all hashes), the four originals need to be re-dispatched. Architect to decide vehicle (likely a fresh request_bugfix or assign_scenarios depending on whether BC has any partial work).

ALSO: BC's working tree has uncommitted untracked files (features/, src/, tests/, walkthrough/) — orthogonal hygiene concern.

ADR-009 v2 FRICTION: this is the first BC work_done landing against the canonized vehicle-(b) workaround. The friction point: the BC's work_done.scenario_hashes echoed the entire original dispatch's hash list when only one was actually pinned. The pattern as canonized does not unambiguously tell the BC which hashes it must observably pin to declare work_done — particularly when the bugfix dispatch is additive vs. replacement. Candidate for ADR-009 v2: spell out the BC's work_done scenario_hashes contract explicitly (must be a strict subset of BC's pinned @scenario_hash set in features/), and clarify whether work_done on a clarify-resolution bead is allowed to claim coverage of unchanged sibling hashes from the original dispatch.

---
◐ lead-km9 · assign adoption-doc scenarios (5) to shopsystem-docs (brief 007 v1)   [● P2 · IN_PROGRESS]
Owner: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-22 · Started: 2026-05-22 · Updated: 2026-05-27

DESCRIPTION
Dispatch all five canonical adoption-doc scenarios from features/docs/ to the newly bootstrapped shopsystem-docs BC. Vehicle: assign_scenarios. Discriminator: new capability — shopsystem-docs has no doc content today (BC was bootstrapped this turn under lead-n78; its repo at https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-docs contains only the poured BC scaffold, no features/, no docs/). Anchors: brief 007 (resolved Q1 single voice agent-consumable, resolved Q2 lead+chosen-BCs end state, resolved Q4 docs-as-BC, boundary 10 v1 plain markdown), ADR-008 (introduces the docs BC), lead-n78 (bootstrap prereq, closed). Brief tracking bead in templates BC tracker: shopsystem-templates-h22. Scenario set: (1) entry doc names framework / prereqs / next step; (2) walkthrough end state is lead-shop + per-BC containers + adopter can dispatch; (3) v1 publishes plain markdown only (no static-site generator); (4) walkthrough structured for agent execution (fenced code blocks, literal arguments, verification steps, no human-vs-agent forks); (5) launcher gap (Q7) named honestly until a host-side lead-launch primitive ships. This bead's ID is the work_id on the wire.

NOTES
2026-05-27 RECONCILIATION (post lead-b34 work_done):

- b0fe0666bcaa2398 (end-to-end runtime) is SUPERSEDED by 56babfaeec4514a3 via clarify-resolution bead lead-b34 (closed 2026-05-27, BC's BDD passes 1/1 for replacement scenario).
- BC has NOT delivered the four unchanged originals (7ac28115, 6055501e, 8c55526c, 3874a7f3): they are echoed in lead-b34's work_done.scenario_hashes but not actually pinned in shopsystem-docs features/ (only adoption_walkthrough_terminal_section.feature exists, pinning 56babfaeec4514a3 alone).
- No work_done on the lead-km9 work_id itself — BC outbox for lead-km9 contains only its prior clarify.
- LEAVING lead-km9 OPEN pending follow-up filed in lead-apk (P1). lead-apk will decide whether BC is still in flight on the four originals or whether they need re-dispatch.
- ADR-009 v2 friction logged in lead-apk: BC may have copied scenario_hashes from dispatch payload without verifying each hash is observably pinned in features/. Canonization candidate.

7	[findings/from-prototype-1.md §5](../findings/from-prototype-1.md) (vehicle
8	selection by pre-state).
9	**Related beads:** `lead-d6o` (clarify protocol gap), `lead-8z1` (multiple
10	clarify rows per work_id), `lead-rv9` (work_done(blocked) lead-side response),
11	`lead-4wy` (catalog work_id pattern asymmetry).
12	**Depends on:** [ADR-006](006-messaging-name-registry-design.md) (the
13	message-registry shape into which a future primitive would slot).
14	
15	## Context
16	
17	When a BC emits `clarify` on a lead dispatch, the spec's §4 catalog lists no
18	`clarify_response` message type. Empirically verified 2026-05-27:
19	
20	```
21	$ shop-msg send --help
22	usage: shop-msg send [-h]
23	                     {request_maintenance,assign_scenarios,request_bugfix} ...
24	```
25	
26	— `shop-msg send` exposes only the three outbound vehicles a lead uses to
27	introduce or tighten work. `shop-msg respond clarify` is BC-side direction
28	(the BC writes its question into the lead's inbox), confirmed by:
29	
30	```
31	$ shop-msg respond clarify --help
32	usage: shop-msg respond clarify [-h] --bc BC --work-id WORK_ID
33	                                --question QUESTION
34	```
35	
36	The `--bc` flag names the BC writing the response — i.e., the caller is the
37	BC, not the lead. The CLI has no lead-side counterpart to "answer a clarify
38	in-band."
39	
40	Compounding the gap: even if a `clarify_response` primitive existed,
41	`repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/shop_msg/storage.py:210-223` enforces a
42	strict "one inbox row per (bc, work_id)" gate on the lead-to-BC direction
43	when `allow_multi_type=False` (the default for sends). The storage layer
44	already carries an `allow_multi_type` flag (lines 178, 186-191) which is set
45	by BC-to-lead writes so multiple message types (`work_done`,
46	`mechanism_observation`, `clarify`) can coexist for the same work_id in the
47	lead inbox; the lead-to-BC send path does not currently opt into that
48	relaxation, so a hypothetical `clarify_response` row would collide with the
49	original `assign_scenarios` row on the same `work_id`.
50	
51	The de-facto precedent for resolving a BC clarify is already established:
52	the `lead-bxl` bead (closed 2026-05-22) resolved
53	`shopsystem-templates-918`'s BC clarify by dispatching a fresh
54	`request_bugfix` on a new lead-side bead carrying the resolving scenario
55	(scenario 08, missing-`.claude.json` warn-and-proceed). The original `lead-gua`
56	(work_id `shopsystem-templates-918`) `assign_scenarios` row remains in
57	flight on the wire; the new bugfix is *additive*, not a replacement. See the
58	`lead-d6o` bead's NOTES entry (2026-05-22) for the empirical confirmation
59	that this pattern works end-to-end.
60	
61	## Decision
62	
63	Adopt a two-layer position:
64	
65	**Layer (b) — operational, in effect immediately.** The lead resolves a BC
66	`clarify` by dispatching a fresh outbound message on a *new lead-side bead*,
67	carrying the resolving scenario set or maintenance instruction in the
68	appropriate vehicle per the standard discriminator:
69	
70	- BC clarify pertaining to **unpinned existing behavior** → `request_bugfix`
71	  on a new lead bead, carrying the tightening scenario(s).
72	- BC clarify pertaining to **new capability** the original dispatch did not
73	  yet name → `assign_scenarios` on a new lead bead, carrying the additional
74	  scenarios.
75	- BC clarify pertaining to a **flat correction** with no scenario change →
76	  `request_maintenance` on a new lead bead.
77	
78	The dispatch description must reference the original work_id (the one that
79	elicited the clarify) so the BC can correlate. The new bead's ID is the
80	work_id on the wire. The original dispatch remains in flight; the BC's
81	existing clarify row in the lead inbox is consumed by the architect once
82	the resolving dispatch is on the wire (per existing
83	`shop-msg consume` discipline).
84	
85	**Layer (a) — durable, deferred to a future implementation.** A
86	`shop-msg send clarify_response --bc <name> --work-id <id> --resolution <text>
87	[--scenario-file ...]` primitive is the longer-term shape. It requires:
88	
89	1. A new `ClarifyResponse` Pydantic schema in `src/catalog/`.
90	2. The lead-to-BC send path opting into `allow_multi_type=True` for
91	   `clarify_response` so the row can coexist with the original
92	   `assign_scenarios` / `request_bugfix` / `request_maintenance` row on the
93	   same work_id.
94	3. A BC-side semantics decision: does receipt of `clarify_response` re-open
95	   the original dispatch for re-processing, or does it stand alone as a
96	   pinned answer the BC reads alongside the original? The `lead-d6o` bead
97	   itemizes this design question; the answer dictates whether the original
98	   row's lifecycle changes when the response lands.
99	
100	Path (a) is not blocked on path (b); they are complementary. Path (a) is
101	deferred because the storage gate relaxation, the catalog schema addition,
102	and the BC-side re-processing semantics are a coordinated change that wants
103	its own brief/PDR cycle, and the operational pressure to ship it has not
104	yet exceeded the friction of path (b).
105	
106	## Alternatives considered
107	
108	**Option A — Implement `shop-msg send clarify_response` now.** Rejected for
109	the immediate window. The change is non-trivial (catalog schema + storage
110	gate widening + BC-side semantics + tests) and the existing workaround is
111	already in routine use without observable failure. Deferring buys time to
112	batch this with the other catalog-protocol-gap beads (`lead-d6o`,
113	`lead-8z1`, `lead-rv9`, `lead-4wy`) into a coordinated brief revision.
114	
115	**Option C — New message type `clarify_resolved` signaling "process the
116	originally-dispatched scenarios now."** Listed as design question #3 in
117	`lead-d6o`. Rejected as the standalone vehicle for this ADR because it
118	overlaps with option A; either path subsumes it. Folded into the future
119	brief that resolves path (a).
120	
121	**Option D — Carry the original scenarios forward in every follow-up
122	dispatch.** Avoids the storage-collision concern by reusing the existing
123	vehicles' carry-forward semantics. Rejected because it produces wire-form
124	hash drift between dispatches (the feature-title wrapping changes between
125	canonical renders), making BDD attestation harder to track and inflating
126	the on-wire payload. `lead-d6o` documents the hash-drift symptom from
127	`lead-1pi` (scenario 57 hash was `d4b8aac19b34ea8c` in `lead-xkh`,
128	`1621b59b0ea8b20b` in `lead-1pi`).
129	
130	## Consequences
131	
132	- The de-facto pattern already in use (per `lead-bxl`) is now canonical.
133	  Future lead-shop turns resolving a BC clarify file a new lead-side bead
134	  and dispatch via the appropriate existing vehicle; this is no longer a
135	  "workaround," it is the documented procedure until path (a) ships.
136	- Lead-shop role templates (`lead-architect.md`) should learn the
137	  clarify-resolution dispatch shape: cite the original work_id in the new
138	  bead's description, scope as "additive — original dispatch remains in
139	  flight," and use the standard discriminator on the resolving content.
140	  A follow-up bead tracks this template edit.
141	- The storage gate at `storage.py:210-223` is the load-bearing dependency
142	  for path (a). Any future PDR that proposes a `clarify_response` primitive
143	  must widen this gate or set `allow_multi_type=True` for the new
144	  message type's lead-to-BC writes.
145	- The `lead-d6o` bead is **not closed** by this ADR. It tracks the deeper
146	  design question of multi-turn clarify semantics; this ADR commits the
147	  operational vehicle but defers the underlying protocol shape.
148	- `mechanism_observation` from a BC reporting a different kind of
149	  protocol gap (e.g., the cross-tracker work_id pattern observed with
150	  `shopsystem-templates-918`) is unaffected by this decision; it routes
151	  per the standing rules in the lead-primer.
152
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-27 17:01:32Z)**_

Good — ADR-009 is clear on vehicle (b) but does NOT specify what `work_done.scenario_hashes` must contain on the clarify-resolution side. That's the v2 gap.

## Step 3: Decide vehicle for the gap closure

I'll go with **vehicle (a) — `assign_scenarios`** on a fresh lead bead. Reasoning:
- BC's pre-state for these 4 scenarios: behavior **not present** in BC code/features. Empirically verified: only `56babfaeec4514a3` is pinned; no other features files exist.
- The BC has no partial implementation to "fix" — there's nothing to tighten. There's just net-new pinning + step-def work to do.
- Q1 of the discriminator ("does the BC have the capability at all?") is **no** for these 4. → `assign_scenarios`.

The user's router-preference agrees with the discriminator outcome. Vehicle (a) it is.

## Step 4: File the new bead and dispatch

# ADR-010 — Clarify-resolution `work_done.scenario_hashes` scope: strict subset of the resolving dispatch, not of the original

**Status:** accepted (2026-05-27)
**Authors:** dstengle, Claude (lead-architect)
**Anchored to:** [ADR-009](009-clarify-resolution-vehicle.md) (clarify-resolution
vehicle: re-dispatch via existing catalog message types on a fresh lead-side
bead); [§4 spec catalog](../04-message-catalog.md); [§4.4 closure
narrative](../04-message-catalog.md).
**Related beads:** `lead-km9` (original assign_scenarios, 5 scenarios),
`lead-b34` (clarify-resolution dispatch, 1 replacement scenario),
`lead-apk` (reconciliation finding that triggered this ADR),
`lead-eqx` (re-dispatch of the four unpinned originals — supersedes lead-apk).
**Amends:** ADR-009 (does not supersede; ADR-009's vehicle decision stands).

## Context

ADR-009 canonized vehicle (b): a BC `clarify` is resolved by dispatching a
fresh outbound message on a new lead-side bead carrying the resolving
scenario set or maintenance instruction. The original dispatch remains in
flight; the new bead is additive.

ADR-009 does not specify what the BC's `work_done.scenario_hashes` field
should contain when the work_done is emitted against the new (resolving)
bead's work_id. The omission produced an observable reconciliation
failure on the first BC work_done landing against the canonized pattern:

**Empirical record (2026-05-27):**

- `lead-km9` (2026-05-22) dispatched 5 adoption-doc scenarios to
  `shopsystem-docs`: `7ac28115fe1a2a33`, `b0fe0666bcaa2398`,
  `6055501e8a23a652`, `8c55526c7a83bcd0`, `3874a7f3c34e4f60`.
- The BC's `clarify` (per lead-km9) flagged `b0fe0666bcaa2398` as not
  establishable in its pytest-bdd harness; said nothing about the other
  four.
- `lead-b34` (clarify-resolution per ADR-009 vehicle (b)) dispatched a
  single replacement scenario (`56babfaeec4514a3`) on a fresh lead bead,
  framed as additive to lead-km9.
- BC's `work_done` on `lead-b34` (2026-05-27) echoed all five hashes in
  `scenario_hashes:`: `56babfaeec4514a3` plus the four unchanged
  originals. The summary text said "Echoing new hash 56babfaeec4514a3
  plus the four unchanged hashes from lead-km9".
- **Observed BC state:** `repos/shopsystem-docs/features/` contains
  exactly one file, pinning `56babfaeec4514a3` only. The four echoed
  originals are not in the BC's features tree at all. `lead-km9` has no
  recorded `work_done`.

The BC reasonably (but incorrectly) interpreted "echo all unchanged
hashes from the original dispatch" as the contract for a
clarify-resolution work_done. Two interpretations were available; both
are defensible against ADR-009's text:

1. **Strict-subset reading:** `scenario_hashes` lists what the BC has
   actually pinned in `features/` and discharged by this work_done.
   Sibling hashes from the original dispatch that the BC has not yet
   pinned must NOT be echoed; they remain in flight against the
   original dispatch's work_id.
2. **Full-original-set reading:** since the resolving dispatch is
   additive to the original, the work_done can echo the full original
   hash set the BC believes itself to be discharging across both
   dispatches, treating one work_done as terminating both work_ids.

The BC followed interpretation (2). The interpretation produces a
mismatch between the wire-form claim and the on-disk reality: the
work_done asserts coverage of scenarios that have no corresponding
`@scenario_hash` tag in any feature file in the BC.

## Decision

**Strict-subset is the canonical rule.** Specifically:

1. A `work_done.scenario_hashes` list MUST be exactly the set of hashes
   the BC has pinned in `features/` AND discharged (BDD passes,
   reviewer-attested) as part of completing the dispatch whose work_id
   the work_done addresses.

2. For a clarify-resolution work_done specifically: `scenario_hashes`
   MUST be limited to hashes carried in the resolving dispatch's
   payload. A clarify-resolution work_done does NOT extend coverage to
   sibling hashes from the original dispatch — even when the resolving
   dispatch is framed as additive to the original.

3. The original dispatch's work_id remains separately open until its
   own work_done lands, covering its own hashes (minus any that were
   superseded by the clarify-resolution). A "superseded" hash is one the
   clarify-resolution dispatch explicitly replaces (e.g., the
   replacement scenario `56babfaeec4514a3` supersedes the original
   `b0fe0666bcaa2398`). The lead's bead notes are the authoritative
   record of supersession.

4. **Observable evidence requirement (BC-side):** before emitting a
   work_done, the BC MUST verify each hash in `scenario_hashes` is
   present in its `features/` tree under an `@scenario_hash:<hash>` tag.
   The verification is a discrete pre-emit step. The Reviewer template
   carried by `shopsystem-templates` is the natural place to encode the
   check; an ADR-010 follow-up bead tracks that template edit (filed
   separately, not dispatched this turn).

5. **Reconciliation-side (lead Architect):** when a work_done lands, the
   reconciliation step MUST grep `features/` in the BC repo for each
   echoed hash. A hash present in `scenario_hashes` but absent from
   `features/` is a protocol violation — file a follow-up lead-side
   bead, do not silently close the original dispatch.

## Alternatives considered

**Option A — Adopt the full-original-set reading.** Rejected. The
work_done's `scenario_hashes` field's value-add is reconciliation; if it
can echo hashes the BC has not actually pinned, the reconciliation step
collapses to "trust the BC's text" and the lead Architect's grep-against-
`features/` check becomes redundant theatre. The strict-subset rule
preserves the field's verification utility.

**Option B — Amend ADR-009 in-place with a v2 section.** Rejected for
this turn because the scope rule is conceptually layered on top of
ADR-009 (vehicle selection) rather than altering it: ADR-009 still
governs which message type the resolving dispatch uses; ADR-010 governs
what the resolving work_done claims. Keeping them as separate documents
makes the dependency explicit (ADR-010 "Amends" ADR-009) and avoids
mutating an accepted ADR's body. If a future synthesis ADR consolidates
the clarify-resolution rule set, both 009 and 010 can be superseded
together.

**Option C — Add a `superseded_hashes` companion field to work_done.**
Rejected as out of scope. Encoding supersession in the message catalog
is a coordinated schema change; the lead's bead notes already carry the
supersession record adequately for the current scale. Revisit if
reconciliation friction becomes routine.

## Consequences

- The BC Reviewer template carried by `shopsystem-templates` should
  learn the pre-emit hash-presence check (grep `features/` for each
  hash in `work_done.scenario_hashes` before emit; abort if any hash is
  absent). Follow-up bead tracks the template edit; not dispatched this
  turn (per the user's standing scope guidance on this turn).
- The lead Architect's reconciliation procedure tightens: every work_done
  reception runs the grep cross-check on the BC's `features/` tree
  before closing the originating bead. A mismatch produces a follow-up
  bead (as `lead-apk` was filed against the `lead-b34` mismatch).
- `lead-km9` (the original dispatch) remains open. Its eventual closure
  path: either (a) its own work_done lands covering its remaining four
  hashes — now superseded by `lead-eqx`'s wire-form hashes due to
  canonical-body drift, so this path is unlikely; or (b) a future
  reconciliation bead closes it as superseded by `lead-eqx` once
  `lead-eqx`'s work_done lands and reconciles cleanly. Path (b) is the
  expected route.
- `lead-b34`'s already-landed work_done is grandfathered. ADR-010
  applies prospectively; the past work_done is the canonized pattern's
  birth pain, not a violation under the rule as it existed at the time.
- The lead-shop role template (`lead-architect.md`) inherits a sharper
  reconciliation procedure: grep-each-hash against BC `features/` on
  every work_done reception. The lead-architect inline template at
  `.claude/agents/lead-architect.md` already requires the
  `@scenario_hash` enumeration step on dispatch (the
  "Have you enumerated the BC's pinned @scenario_hash set" check) —
  ADR-010 generalizes the same enumeration discipline to the
  reconciliation side. A follow-up bead can edit the canonical template
  in `shopsystem-templates` to make the reconciliation grep explicit;
  not dispatched this turn.

```

◐ lead-i18 · assign_scenarios: shopsystem-messaging — shop-msg implicit CWD-based shop resolution   [● P1 · IN_PROGRESS]
Owner: David Stenglein · Assignee: David Stenglein · Type: task
Created: 2026-05-27 · Started: 2026-05-27 · Updated: 2026-05-27

DESCRIPTION
Add implicit shop resolution to shop-msg: when called without --bc/--lead, walk up from CWD looking for .claude/shop/name.md and .claude/shop/type.md. Read type.md to choose BC vs lead mode; read name.md to resolve the canonical name; use the registry to find the path as today. Explicit --bc <name> / --lead <name> remain unchanged for cross-shop addressing. This eliminates the need for the canonical BC settings.json SessionStart hook to embed the shop name. Anchors: PDR-007 (name-based addressing — this extends it with a CWD-implicit form, does not reverse it). Prior art: bd discovers .beads/ from CWD using the same walk-up pattern. Discriminator: NEW capability — implicit CWD resolution does not exist today; calling shop-msg prime with no addressing flags currently errors via the mutually-exclusive arg group. Vehicle: assign_scenarios. PO must author scenarios pinning: (a) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a BC when CWD is inside a BC shop dir, (b) bare 'shop-msg prime' resolves a lead when CWD is inside a lead shop dir, (c) error case when CWD is not inside any shop, (d) explicit --bc/--lead still works unchanged. Suggested generalization to other subcommands (pending, respond, watch) — PO judgment whether to bundle.
